ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: fix(git): add checked-out branch support to `gbg*` (#12397)
removed_lines: -alias gbgd='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -d'
-alias gbgD='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -D'
added_lines: +alias gbgd='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | cut -c 3- | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -d'
+alias gbgD='LANG=C git branch --no-color -vv | grep ": gone\]" | cut -c 3- | awk '"'"'{print $1}'"'"' | xargs git branch -D'

fix(git): fix `gbda` alias when there are no merged branches (#10005)
removed_lines: -alias gbda='git branch --no-color --merged | command grep -vE "^(\+|\*|\s*($(git_main_branch)|$(git_develop_branch))\s*$)" | command xargs -n 1 git branch -d'

* feat: added variable session duration, if the role to be assumed permits it.

* bug: incorrect assigment for session length

* fix: profile extraction failed with some versions of sed

Fixed the issue that resulted from merging upstream changes to allow "." in the profile name

removed_lines: -      acpi 2>/dev/null | command grep -v "rate information unavailable" | command grep -E '^Battery.*(Disc|C)harging' | cut -f2 -d ',' | tr -cd '[:digit:]'

Remove invalid batteries (#8275)

+      acpi 2>/dev/null | command grep -v "rate information unavailable" | command grep -E '^Battery.*(Disc|C)harging' | cut -f2 -d ',' | tr -cd '[:digit:]'
+      acpi 2>/dev/null | command grep -v "rate information unavailable" | cut -f3 -d ','

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Report only active battery (#4726)

On a system with multiple batteries (like thinkpads) report percentage
and time remaining only for the active battery (the one being
discharged).

Ideally we should report all batteries, but acpi only shows time remaining
for the active battery. Also callers of these functions expect a single
return value. This is still better than reporting 596% remaining (like it
did on my laptop).

For the reference, the output of acpi command with multiple batteries looks
like this:

    Battery 0: Unknown, 5%
    Battery 1: Discharging, 86%, 03:14:04 remaining

systemadmin: fix header line sorting in pscpu (#6167)

Inside `pscpu` and `pscpu10` aliases, remove sorting of the header line.
homestead: repair sed regex (#8103)

plugins/git: Fix `gbda` trying to delete worktree branches

Git learned to add a `+` in front of branches that are
checked out in other worktrees.

See: https://github.com/git/git/blob/745f6812895b31c02b29bdfe4ae8e5498f776c26/Documentation/RelNotes/2.23.0.txt#L252-L256
removed_lines: -alias gbda='git branch --no-color --merged | command grep -vE "^(\*|\s*(master|develop|dev)\s*$)" | command xargs -n 1 git branch -d'
added_lines: +alias gbda='git branch --no-color --merged | command grep -vE "^(\+|\*|\s*(master|develop|dev)\s*$)" | command xargs -n 1 git branch -d'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: feature: add condition for regular expression
change key string

installer: make sure shell in /etc/shells is not commented

Otherwise we risk a situation where a full path to `zsh` is commented, i.e.:

    #/usr/local/bin/zsh

emacs: use open frame if there is one present

Fixes #5779
Fixes #5405

git: delete branches in `gbda` only if there are any (#6079)

It doesn't make sense to run `git branch -d $BRANCH` if the `$BRANCH` is empty.
removed_lines: -alias gbda='git branch --no-color --merged | command grep -vE "^(\*|\s*(master|develop|dev)\s*$)" | command xargs -n 1 git branch -d'
added_lines: +alias gbda='git branch --no-color --merged | command grep -vE "^(\*|\s*(master|develop|dev)\s*$)" | command xargs -r -n 1 git branch -d'

defined.

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: systemadmin: refactor plugin and fix README (#7295)

Use existing ssh-agent when invoking a sudo shell (#3891)

When invoking a shell as root using ```sudo -s```, the ssh-agent plugin
starts a new agent although it already exists.

The problem boils down to a check if ssh-agent is running using
```ps x```. If that is extended to ```ps ax``` for root, then the
existing ssh-agent will still work.
allow . in profile names

The mix command for listing all available actions has been changed from
`mix --help` to `mix help`.

-        -Dit.test= `if [ -d ./src/test/java ] ; then find ./src/test/java -type f -name '*.java' | grep -v svn | sed 's?.*/\([^/]*\)\..*?-Dit.test=\1?' ; fi`

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
ssh-agent: check ssh-agent process w/ ps again

The alternative is using tools that aren't available everywhere.
The latest report is that cygwin/msys2 doesn't have pgrep.

Fixes #5418.

* Fix battery plugin when acpi writes to stderr
* Make stderr redirection in battery plugin more idiomatic
Fix git_prompt_status() not showing ahead/behind/diverged status correctly (#5388)

ssh-agent: fix non-standard process check w/ pgrep

Confirmed to work on MacOS, OpenBSD, Solaris and busybox.
Simplify PID check of current ssh-agent

* Fix gbda alias to support git config color.ui = always

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
Remove warning lines from phing completion

Remove "Warning:" lines from phing targets list. Warning lines are
returned by phing when a target has no tasks or dependencies.

# Conflicts:
#	plugins/phing/phing.plugin.zsh
[Composer] redirect stderr to /dev/null for completion commands
Accept single and double quotes in Gulp plugin
removed_lines: -    compls=$(grep -Eo "gulp.task\(('(([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)',)" gulpfile.js 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo "'(([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)'" | sed s/"'"//g | sort)
added_lines: +    compls=$(grep -Eo "gulp.task\((['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"],)" gulpfile.js 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo "['\"](([a-zA-Z0-9]|-)*)['\"]" | sed s/"['\"]"//g | sort)"

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message: 'ip' command compatibility and much quicker external IP detection
removed_lines: -    ifconfig  | grep 'inet addr:'| grep -v '127.0.0.1' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}'
added_lines: +        ifconfig  | grep 'inet addr:'| grep -v '127.0.0.1' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}'

ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh.message:
plugin symfony2 sf2.7 compatibility fix

with symfony 2.7 command group titles are listed as commands. this commit prevents it.
Fix awk command. \s only working in gawk
Fixes gradle autocomplete. Regex did not include subproject tasks before.
Fix: "\s" is a gawk-specific regexp operator.
Laravel 5 support
removed_lines: -	php artisan --no-ansi | sed "1,/Available commands/d" | awk '/^  [a-z]+/ { print $1 }'
added_lines: +	php artisan --no-ansi | sed "1,/Available commands/d" | awk '/^ +[a-z]+/ { print $1 }'

`\d` in sed regular expressions doesn't work (see http://stackoverflow.com/questions/14671293/why-does-d-doesnt-work-in-regular-expression-in-sed)
use ls instead of find to avoid incompatibility with gnu find

are usually just warnings
